tagline: Work with Intercom workspace data from the command line.

groups.title: Content, Help Center, News, and Tickets

global_flags.name: credential
global_flags.summary: Use a saved Intercom connector credential.

global_flags.name: max-bytes
global_flags.summary: Maximum bytes for bounded direct reads.

flags.name: display-avatar
flags.summary: If set to true, the response will include the admin's avatar object containing the image URL. Defaults to false.

commands.risk: high: Set an admin to away; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. This action also requires --confirm destructive when executing an approved plan.

flags.name: admin-id
flags.summary: admin id path value.

flags.name: away-mode-enabled
flags.summary: Set to "true" to change the status of the admin to away.

flags.name: away-mode-reassign
flags.summary: Set to "true" to assign any new conversation replies to your default inbox.

flags.name: away-status-reason-id
flags.summary: The unique identifier of the away status reason

flags.name: admin-id
flags.summary: admin id path value.

flags.name: created-at-after
flags.summary: The start date that you request data for. It must be formatted as a UNIX timestamp.

flags.name: created-at-before
flags.summary: The end date that you request data for. It must be formatted as a UNIX timestamp.

commands.risk: high: Create a content import source; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. This action also requires --confirm destructive when executing an approved plan.

flags.name: sync-behavior
flags.summary: If you intend to create or update External Pages via the API, this should be set to `api`.

flags.name: status
flags.summary: The status of the content import source.

flags.name: url
flags.summary: The URL of the content import source.

commands.risk: high: Delete a content import source; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. This action also requires --confirm destructive when executing an approved plan.

flags.name: source-id
flags.summary: source id path value.

commands.risk: high: Update a content import source; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. This action also requires --confirm destructive when executing an approved plan.

flags.name: source-id
flags.summary: source id path value.

flags.name: sync-behavior
flags.summary: If you intend to create or update External Pages via the API, this should be set to `api`. You can not change the value to or from api.

flags.name: status
flags.summary: The status of the content import source.

flags.name: url
flags.summary: The URL of the content import source. This may only be different from the existing value if the sync behavior is API.

flags.name: source-id
flags.summary: source id path value.

commands.risk: high: Delete an external page; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. This action also requires --confirm destructive when executing an approved plan.

flags.name: page-id
flags.summary: page id path value.

commands.summary: Create an external page (or update an external page by external ID)
commands.risk: medium: Create an external page (or update an external page by external ID); live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

flags.name: title
flags.summary: The title of the external page.

flags.name: html
flags.summary: The body of the external page in HTML.

flags.name: url
flags.summary: The URL of the external page. This will be used by Fin to link end users to the page it based its answer on. When a URL is not present, Fin will not reference the source.

flags.name: ai-agent-availability
flags.summary: Whether the external page should be used to answer questions by AI Agent. Will not default when updating an existing external page.

flags.name: ai-copilot-availability
flags.summary: Whether the external page should be used to answer questions by AI Copilot. Will not default when updating an existing external page.

flags.name: locale
flags.summary: Always en

flags.name: source-id
flags.summary: The unique identifier for the source of the external page which was given by Intercom. Every external page must be associated with a Content Import Source which represents the place it comes from and from which it inherits a default audience (configured in the UI). For a new source, make a POST request to the Content Import Source endpoint and an ID for the source will be returned in the response.

flags.name: external-id
flags.summary: The identifier for the external page which was given by the source. Must be unique for the source.

commands.risk: medium: Update an external page; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

flags.name: page-id
flags.summary: page id path value.

flags.name: title
flags.summary: The title of the external page.

flags.name: html
flags.summary: The body of the external page in HTML.

flags.name: url
flags.summary: The URL of the external page. This will be used by Fin to link end users to the page it based its answer on.

flags.name: fin-availability
flags.summary: Whether the external page should be used to answer questions by Fin.

flags.name: locale
flags.summary: Always en

flags.name: source-id
flags.summary: The unique identifier for the source of the external page which was given by Intercom. Every external page must be associated with a Content Import Source which represents the place it comes from and from which it inherits a default audience (configured in the UI). For a new source, make a POST request to the Content Import Source endpoint and an ID for the source will be returned in the response.

flags.name: external-id
flags.summary: The identifier for the external page which was given by the source. Must be unique for the source.

flags.name: page-id
flags.summary: page id path value.

commands.risk: high: Create an article; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. This action also requires --confirm destructive when executing an approved plan.

flags.name: title
flags.summary: The title of the article.For multilingual articles, this will be the title of the default language's content.

flags.name: description
flags.summary: The description of the article. For multilingual articles, this will be the description of the default language's content.

flags.name: body
flags.summary: The content of the article. For multilingual articles, this will be the body of the default language's content.

flags.name: author-id
flags.summary: The id of the author of the article. For multilingual articles, this will be the id of the author of the default language's content. Must be a teammate on the help center's workspace.

flags.name: state
flags.summary: Whether the article will be `published` or will be a `draft`. Defaults to draft. For multilingual articles, this will be the state of the default language's content.

flags.name: parent-id
flags.summary: The id of the article's parent collection or section. An article without this field stands alone.

flags.name: parent-type
flags.summary: The type of parent, which can either be a `collection` or `section`.

flags.name: translated-content
flags.summary: The Translated Content of an Article. The keys are the locale codes and the values are the translated content of the article.

commands.risk: high: Delete an article; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. This action also requires --confirm destructive when executing an approved plan.

flags.name: article-id
flags.summary: article id path value.

flags.name: phrase
flags.summary: The phrase within your articles to search for.

flags.name: state
flags.summary: The state of the Articles returned. One of `published`, `draft` or `all`.

flags.name: help-center-id
flags.summary: The ID of the Help Center to search in.

flags.name: highlight
flags.summary: Return a highlighted version of the matching content within your articles. Refer to the response schema for more details.

commands.risk: high: Update an article; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. This action also requires --confirm destructive when executing an approved plan.

flags.name: article-id
flags.summary: article id path value.

flags.name: title
flags.summary: The title of the article.For multilingual articles, this will be the title of the default language's content.

flags.name: description
flags.summary: The description of the article. For multilingual articles, this will be the description of the default language's content.

flags.name: body
flags.summary: The content of the article. For multilingual articles, this will be the body of the default language's content.

flags.name: author-id
flags.summary: The id of the author of the article. For multilingual articles, this will be the id of the author of the default language's content. Must be a teammate on the help center's workspace.

flags.name: state
flags.summary: Whether the article will be `published` or will be a `draft`. Defaults to draft. For multilingual articles, this will be the state of the default language's content.

flags.name: parent-id
flags.summary: The id of the article's parent collection or section. An article without this field stands alone.

flags.name: parent-type
flags.summary: The type of parent, which can either be a `collection` or `section`.

flags.name: translated-content
flags.summary: The Translated Content of an Article. The keys are the locale codes and the values are the translated content of the article.

flags.name: article-id
flags.summary: article id path value.

flags.name: page
flags.summary: The page of results to fetch. Defaults to first page

flags.name: per-page
flags.summary: How many results to display per page. Defaults to 25. Max 25.

flags.name: call-id
flags.summary: call id path value.

commands.summary: Get call recording by call id

flags.name: call-id
flags.summary: call id path value.

flags.name: conversation-id
flags.summary: Conversation id whose calls should be listed.

commands.summary: Get call transcript by call id

flags.summary: call id path value.

commands.risk: high: Delete a company; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. This action also requires --confirm destructive when executing an approved plan.

flags.name: company-id
flags.summary: company id path value.

flags.name: name
flags.summary: The `name` of the company to filter by.

flags.name: company-id
flags.summary: The `company_id` of the company to filter by.

flags.name: tag-id
flags.summary: The `tag_id` of the company to filter by.

flags.name: segment-id
flags.summary: The `segment_id` of the company to filter by.

flags.name: page
flags.summary: The page of results to fetch. Defaults to first page

flags.name: per-page
flags.summary: How many results to display per page. Defaults to 15

commands.risk: medium: Create or Update a company; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

flags.name: name
flags.summary: The name of the Company

flags.name: company-id
flags.summary: The company id you have defined for the company. Can't be updated

flags.name: plan
flags.summary: The name of the plan you have associated with the company.

flags.name: size
flags.summary: The number of employees in this company.

flags.name: website
flags.summary: The URL for this company's website. Please note that the value specified here is not validated. Accepts any string.

flags.name: industry
flags.summary: The industry that this company operates in.

flags.name: custom-attributes
flags.summary: A hash of key/value pairs containing any other data about the company you want Intercom to store.

flags.name: remote-created-at
flags.summary: The time the company was created by you.

flags.name: monthly-spend
flags.summary: How much revenue the company generates for your business. Note that this will truncate floats. i.e. it only allow for whole integers, 155.98 will be truncated to 155. Note that this has an upper limit of 2**31-1 or 2147483647..

commands.risk: medium: Update a company; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

flags.name: plan

flags.summary: The number of employees in this company.

flags.name: website
flags.summary: The URL for this company's website. Please note that the value specified here is not validated. Accepts any string.

flags.name: industry
flags.summary: The industry that this company operates in.

flags.name: custom-attributes
flags.summary: A hash of key/value pairs containing any other data about the company you want Intercom to store.

flags.name: monthly-spend
flags.summary: How much revenue the company generates for your business. Note that this will truncate floats. i.e. it only allow for whole integers, 155.98 will be truncated to 155. Note that this has an upper limit of 2**31-1 or 2147483647..

flags.name: company-id
flags.summary: company id path value.

flags.name: company-id
flags.summary: company id path value.

flags.name: page
flags.summary: The page of results to fetch. Defaults to first page

flags.name: per-page
flags.summary: How many results to return per page. Defaults to 15

flags.name: order
flags.summary: `asc` or `desc`. Return the companies in ascending or descending order. Defaults to desc

flags.name: scroll-param
flags.summary: scroll param

flags.summary: company id path value.

commands.risk: medium: Creates a conversation; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

flags.name: from
flags.summary: from

flags.name: body
flags.summary: The content of the message. HTML is not supported.

flags.name: subject
flags.summary: The title of the email. Only applicable if the message type is email.

flags.name: attachment-urls
flags.summary: A list of image URLs that will be added as attachments. You can include up to 10 URLs.

flags.name: created-at
flags.summary: The time the conversation was created as a UTC Unix timestamp. If not provided, the current time will be used. This field is only recommneded for migrating past conversations from another source into Intercom.

commands.risk: high: Delete a conversation; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. This action also requires --confirm destructive when executing an approved plan.

flags.name: conversation-id
flags.summary: conversation id path value.

flags.name: per-page
flags.summary: How many results per page

flags.name: starting-after
flags.summary: String used to get the next page of conversations.

commands.risk: medium: Update a conversation; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

flags.name: conversation-id
flags.summary: conversation id path value.

flags.name: display-as
flags.summary: Set to plaintext to retrieve conversation messages in plain text.

flags.name: read
flags.summary: Mark a conversation as read within Intercom.

flags.name: title
flags.summary: The title given to the conversation

flags.name: custom-attributes
flags.summary: An object containing the different custom attributes associated to the conversation as key-value pairs. For relationship attributes the value will be a list of custom object instance models. System-defined attributes such as "CX Score rating" and "CX Score explanation" may also be included.

flags.name: company-id
flags.summary: The ID of the company that the conversation is associated with. The unique identifier for the company which is given by Intercom. Set to nil to remove company.

flags.name: conversation-id
flags.summary: conversation id path value.

flags.name: display-as
flags.summary: Set to plaintext to retrieve conversation messages in plain text.

flags.name: include-translations
flags.summary: If set to true, conversation parts will be translated to the detected language of the conversation.

commands.summary: Convert a conversation to a ticket
commands.risk: high: Convert a conversation to a ticket; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. This action also requires --confirm destructive when executing an approved plan.

flags.name: conversation-id
flags.summary: conversation id path value.

flags.name: ticket-type-id
flags.summary: The ID of the type of ticket you want to convert the conversation to

flags.name: attributes
flags.summary: The attributes set on the ticket. When setting the default title and description attributes, the attribute keys that should be used are `_default_title_` and `_default_description_`. When setting ticket type attributes of the list attribute type, the key should be the attribute name and the value of the attribute should be the list item id, obtainable by [listing the ticket type](ref:get_ticket-types). For example, if the ticket type has an attribute called `priority` of type `list`, the key should be `priority` and the value of the attribute should be the guid of the list item (e.g. `de1825a0-0164-4070-8ca6-13e22462fa7e`).

flags.summary: The time the event occurred as a UTC Unix timestamp

flags.name: user-id
flags.summary: Your identifier for the user.

flags.name: id
flags.summary: The unique identifier for the contact (lead or user) which is given by Intercom.

flags.name: email
flags.summary: An email address for your user. An email should only be used where your application uses email to uniquely identify users.

flags.name: metadata
flags.summary: Optional metadata about the event.

flags.summary: String representing the object's type. Always has the value `ip_allowlist`.

flags.summary: Whether the IP allowlist is enabled for the workspace.

flags.name: ip-allowlist
flags.summary:
List of allowed IP addresses and/or IP ranges in CIDR notation.
Examples:
- Single IP: `192.168.0.1`
- IP range: `192.168.0.1/24` (allows 192.168.0.0 - 192.168.0.255)

flags.summary: The title of the email.

flags.name: body
flags.summary: The content of the message. HTML and plaintext are supported.

flags.name: template
flags.summary: The style of the outgoing message. Possible values `plain` or `personal`.

flags.name: from
flags.summary: The sender of the message. If not provided, the default sender will be used.

flags.name: to
flags.summary: A recipient of a message

flags.name: cc
flags.summary: A recipient of a message

flags.name: bcc
flags.summary: A recipient of a message

flags.name: created-at
flags.summary: The time the message was created. If not provided, the current time will be used.

flags.name: create-conversation-without-contact-reply
flags.summary: Whether a conversation should be opened in the inbox for the message without the contact replying. Defaults to false if not provided.

flags.summary: When set to `true`, the news item will appear in the messenger newsfeed without showing a notification badge.

commands.risk: medium: Create a ticket; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

flags.name: ticket-type-id
flags.summary: The ID of the type of ticket you want to create

flags.name: contacts
flags.summary: The list of contacts (users or leads) affected by this ticket. Currently only one is allowed

flags.name: conversation-to-link-id
flags.summary:
The ID of the conversation you want to link to the ticket. Here are the valid ways of linking two tickets:
 - conversation | back-office ticket
 - customer tickets | non-shared back-office ticket
 - conversation | tracker ticket
 - customer ticket | tracker ticket

flags.name: company-id
flags.summary: The ID of the company that the ticket is associated with. The unique identifier for the company which is given by Intercom

flags.name: created-at
flags.summary: The time the ticket was created. If not provided, the current time will be used.

flags.name: ticket-attributes
flags.summary: The attributes set on the ticket. When setting the default title and description attributes, the attribute keys that should be used are `_default_title_` and `_default_description_`. When setting ticket type attributes of the list attribute type, the key should be the attribute name and the value of the attribute should be the list item id, obtainable by [listing the ticket type](ref:get_ticket-types). For example, if the ticket type has an attribute called `priority` of type `list`, the key should be `priority` and the value of the attribute should be the guid of the list item (e.g. `de1825a0-0164-4070-8ca6-13e22462fa7e`).

flags.name: assignment
flags.summary: assignment

commands.risk: high: Delete a ticket; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. This action also requires --confirm destructive when executing an approved plan.

flags.name: ticket-id
flags.summary: ticket id path value.

flags.name: open
flags.summary: Specify if a ticket is open. Set to false to close a ticket. Closing a ticket will also unsnooze it.

flags.name: is-shared
flags.summary: Specify whether the ticket is visible to users.

flags.name: snoozed-until
flags.summary: The time you want the ticket to reopen.

flags.name: admin-id
flags.summary: The ID of the admin performing ticket update. Needed for workflows execution and attributing actions to specific admins.

flags.name: assignee-id
flags.summary: The ID of the admin or team to which the ticket is assigned. Set this 0 to unassign it.

flags.summary: Whether the attribute allows multiple files to be attached to it (only applicable to file attributes)

flags.name: archived
flags.summary: Whether the attribute should be archived and not shown during creation of the ticket (it will still be present on previously created tickets)

commands.risk: high: Convert a visitor; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute. This action also requires --confirm destructive when executing an approved plan.

flags.name: type
flags.summary: Represents the role of the Contact model. Accepts `lead` or `user`.

flags.name: user
flags.summary: The unique identifiers retained after converting or merging.

flags.name: visitor
flags.summary: The unique identifiers to convert a single Visitor.

commands.risk: medium: Update a visitor; live Intercom mutation requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute
commands.approval: Reverse ETL writes require plan, preview, approval, execute.

flags.name: id
flags.summary: A unique identified for the visitor which is given by Intercom.

flags.name: user-id
flags.summary: A unique identified for the visitor which is given by you.

flags.name: name
flags.summary: The visitor's name.

flags.name: custom-attributes
flags.summary: The custom attributes which are set for the visitor.

commands.summary: Retrieve a visitor with User ID

flags.name: user-id
flags.summary: The user_id of the Visitor you want to retrieve.

help_topics.name: writes
help_topics.summary: Reverse ETL write commands only create plans until preview and approval gates are satisfied.

help_topics.name: binary
help_topics.summary: Binary Intercom endpoints return bounded metadata or text; file bodies are redacted by default.
